Peter Hakim, Esq. 213-289-3765 Downtown LA Group for Plaintiff.
Stephanie Drenski, Esq. 415-438-5923 Lewis Brisbois for Defendant.

Argued. Ms. Drenski argues. Mr. Hakim submits to the tentative ruling. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT LYFT, INC. Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.7

Taken Under Submission.***

Judge: RIchard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/rbu)

Off Calendar Dec-18-2023 Continued To Jan-09-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Peter Hakim, Esq. OWNTOWN L.A. LAW GROUP (213)389-3765 Peter@downtownlalaw.com
Attorneys for Plaintiff, ETER ELIAS
Stephanie Drenski, Esq., LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP 415.362.8580
Stephanie.Drenski@lewisbrisbois.com, Attorney Defendant, LYFT, INC.

Hearing held. discussed, not ARGUED. Court hold discussion with counsel present as to Plaintiff's late filed opposition.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Mandatory Relief Pursuant To C.C.P. 473 (B); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Plaintiff'S Counsel Peter Hakim, Esq.
Off Calendar Nov-03-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF PETER ELIAS'S MOTION FOR MANDATORY RELIEF PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 473 (b).

Appearances via Zoom: Peter Hakim of the Downtown LA Law Group (213.389.3765) for plaintiff and moving party; Stephanie Drenski of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (415.362.8580) for defendant and responding party.

Argued and the Court takes the matter under submission. Each side to submit a proposed form of order for the Court's consideration. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Mandatory Relief Pursuant To Ccp Sec 473(B)
Off Calendar Sep-18-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Pater Hakim, Esq. 213-389-3765, DTLA law group for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

ARGUED. The Court announces the contest email was received untimely. Mr. Hakim argues to reconsider content in spite of typo. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF PETER ELIAS' Motion For Mandatory Relief Pursuant To CCP Sec 473(B).

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiff's motion for CCP 473 relief is denied. The motion is directed at "Uber." (See, e.g., Not. ii:5-11; Memo. 1:3, 17, 2:9; Hakim Dec. 14:11-13.) However, Uber is not a party in this case.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-16-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Feb-14-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.'s Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens

Appearances:
Igor Fradkin, Esq. of DOWNTOWN L.A. LAW GROUP, Tel: 213.389.3765, for plaintiff, via ZOOM videoconference;
Anna Gourgiotopoulou, Esq. of LEWIS BRISBOIS, Tel: 415.262.8570, appearing for defendant/moving party LYFT, INC.;
Robert Altomare, Esq. of CESARI, WERNER AND MORIARTY, Tel: 650.991.5126, for defendant/joining party STRATAKOS, via ZOOM videoconference.

Plaintiff not filing an opposition and the timeliness of contesting the tentative ruling were discussed and argued.

The court allowed plaintiff to argue. At the conclusion of argument, the court took the matter under submission.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (302/RBU)

*********************************************************************************************

AFTER HEARING, the Court grants Defendant LYFT Inc.'s Motion To Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens. Order signed. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Joinder In Defendant Lyft, Inc.S Motion To Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Feb-14-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT EPAMINONDAS STRATAKOS' Notice Of Joinder In Defendant Lyft, Inc.S Motion To Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens

Appearances:
Igor Fradkin, Esq. of DOWNTOWN L.A. LAW GROUP, Tel: 213.389.3765, for plaintiff, via ZOOM videoconference;
Anna Gourgiotopoulou, Esq. of LEWIS BRISBOIS, Tel: 415.262.8570, appearing for defendant/moving party LYFT, INC.;
Robert Altomare, Esq. of CESARI, WERNER AND MORIARTY, Tel: 650.991.5126, for defendant/joining party STRATAKOS, via ZOOM videoconference.

Plaintiff not filing an opposition and the timeliness of contesting the tentative ruling were discussed and argued.

The court allowed plaintiff to argue. At the conclusion of argument, the court took the matter under submission.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (302/RBU)

After hearing, the Court signed an order granting Defendant EPAMINONDAS STRATAKOS' Joinder In Defendant Lyft's Motion To Dismiss For Forum Non Conveniens.
